# dburl

```go
import "github.com/xo/dburl"
```

Package dburl provides a standard, [net/url.URL](<https://pkg.go.dev/net/url/#URL>) style mechanism for parsing and opening SQL database connection strings for Go. Provides standardized way to parse and open [URL](<#URL>)'s for popular databases PostgreSQL, MySQL, SQLite3, Oracle Database, Microsoft SQL Server, in addition to most other SQL databases with a publicly available Go driver.

See the [package documentation README section](<https://pkg.go.dev/github.com/xo/dburl#section-readme>) for more details.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
 "log"

 "github.com/xo/dburl"
)

func main() {
 db, err := dburl.Open("my://user:pass@host:1234/dbname")
 if err != nil {
  log.Fatal(err)
 }
 defer db.Close()
 res, err := db.Query("SELECT ...")
 if err != nil {
  log.Fatal(err)
 }
 for res.Next() {
  /* ... */
 }
 if err := res.Err(); err != nil {
  log.Fatal(err)
 }
}
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Parse)</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
 "database/sql"
 "log"

 "github.com/xo/dburl"
)

func main() {
 u, err := dburl.Parse("pg://user:pass@host:1234/dbname")
 if err != nil {
  log.Fatal(err)
 }
 db, err := sql.Open(u.Driver, u.DSN)
 if err != nil {
  log.Fatal(err)
 }
 defer db.Close()
 res, err := db.Query("SELECT ...")
 if err != nil {
  log.Fatal(err)
 }
 for res.Next() {
  /* ... */
 }
 if err := res.Err(); err != nil {
  log.Fatal(err)
 }
}
```

</p>
</details>

## Variables

<a name="OdbcIgnoreQueryPrefixes"></a>OdbcIgnoreQueryPrefixes are the query prefixes to ignore when generating the odbc DSN. Used by [GenOdbc](<#GenOdbc>).

```go
var OdbcIgnoreQueryPrefixes []string
```

<a name="OpenFile"></a>OpenFile is the default open file func.

Used internally to read file headers.

```go
var OpenFile = func(name string) (fs.File, error) {
    return os.OpenFile(name, os.O_RDONLY, 0)
}
```

<a name="ResolveSchemeType"></a>ResolveSchemeType is a configuration setting to open paths on disk using [SchemeType](<#SchemeType>), [Stat](<#Stat>), and [OpenFile](<#OpenFile>). Set this to false in an \`init\(\)\` func in order to disable this behavior.

```go
var ResolveSchemeType = true
```

<a name="Stat"></a>Stat is the default stat func.

Used internally to stat files, and used when generating the DSNs for postgres://, mysql://, file:// schemes, and opaque [URL](<#URL>)'s.

```go
var Stat = func(name string) (fs.FileInfo, error) {
    return fs.Stat(os.DirFS(filepath.Dir(name)), filepath.Base(name))
}
```

<a name="BuildURL"></a>

## func [BuildURL](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/dburl.go#L409>)

```go
func BuildURL(components map[string]any) (string, error)
```

BuildURL creates a dsn using the mapped components.

Recognized components are:

```
protocol, proto, scheme
transport
username, user
password, pass
hostname, host
port
path, file, opaque
database, dbname, db
instance
parameters, params, options, opts, query, q
```

See [BuildURL](<#BuildURL>) for more information.

## func [FileTypes](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/scheme.go#L494>)

```go
func FileTypes() []string
```

FileTypes returns the registered file types.

<a name="Open"></a>

## func [Open](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/dburl.go#L34>)

```go
func Open(urlstr string) (*sql.DB, error)
```

Open takes a URL string, also known as a DSN, in the form of "protocol\+transport://user:pass@host/dbname?option1=a&option2=b" and opens a standard \[sql.DB\] connection.

See [Parse](<#Parse>) for information on formatting URL strings to work properly with Open.

<a name="OpenMap"></a>

## func [OpenMap](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/dburl.go#L49>)

```go
func OpenMap(components map[string]any) (*sql.DB, error)
```

OpenMap takes a map of URL components and opens a standard \[sql.DB\] connection.

See [BuildURL](<#BuildURL>) for information on the recognized map components.

<a name="Error"></a>

## type [Error](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/dburl.go#L347>)

Error is an error.

```go
type Error string
```

<a name="ErrInvalidDatabaseScheme"></a>Error values.

```go
const (
    // ErrInvalidDatabaseScheme is the invalid database scheme error.
    ErrInvalidDatabaseScheme Error = "invalid database scheme"
    // ErrUnknownDatabaseScheme is the unknown database type error.
    ErrUnknownDatabaseScheme Error = "unknown database scheme"
    // ErrUnknownFileHeader is the unknown file header error.
    ErrUnknownFileHeader Error = "unknown file header"
    // ErrUnknownFileExtension is the unknown file extension error.
    ErrUnknownFileExtension Error = "unknown file extension"
    // ErrInvalidTransportProtocol is the invalid transport protocol error.
    ErrInvalidTransportProtocol Error = "invalid transport protocol"
    // ErrRelativePathNotSupported is the relative paths not supported error.
    ErrRelativePathNotSupported Error = "relative path not supported"
    // ErrMissingHost is the missing host error.
    ErrMissingHost Error = "missing host"
    // ErrMissingPath is the missing path error.
    ErrMissingPath Error = "missing path"
    // ErrMissingUser is the missing user error.
    ErrMissingUser Error = "missing user"
    // ErrInvalidQuery is the invalid query error.
    ErrInvalidQuery Error = "invalid query"
)
```

```go
func (err Error) Error() string
```

Error satisfies the error interface.

<a name="Scheme"></a>

## type [Scheme](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/scheme.go#L25-L49>)

Scheme wraps information used for registering a database URL scheme for use with [Parse](<#Parse>)/[Open](<#Open>).

```go
type Scheme struct {
    // Driver is the name of the SQL driver that is set as the Scheme in
    // Parse'd URLs and is the driver name expected by the standard sql.Open
    // calls.
    //
    // Note: a 2 letter alias will always be registered for the Driver as the
    // first 2 characters of the Driver, unless one of the Aliases includes an
    // alias that is 2 characters.
    Driver string
    // Generator is the func responsible for generating a DSN based on parsed
    // URL information.
    //
    // Note: this func should not modify the passed URL.
    Generator func(*URL) (string, string, error)
    // Transport are allowed protocol transport types for the scheme.
    Transport Transport
    // Opaque toggles Parse to not re-process URLs with an "opaque" component.
    Opaque bool
    // Aliases are any additional aliases for the scheme.
    Aliases []string
    // Override is the Go SQL driver to use instead of Driver.
    //
    // Used for "wire compatible" driver schemes.
    Override string
}
```

### func [Unregister](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/scheme.go#L454>)

```go
func Unregister(name string) *Scheme
```

Unregister unregisters a scheme and all associated aliases, returning the removed [Scheme](<#Scheme>).

<a name="Transport"></a>

## type [Transport](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/scheme.go#L12>)

Transport is the allowed transport protocol types in a database [URL](<#URL>) scheme.

```go
type Transport uint
```

<a name="TransportNone"></a>Transport types.

```go
const (
    TransportNone Transport = 0
    TransportTCP  Transport = 1
    TransportUDP  Transport = 2
    TransportUnix Transport = 4
    TransportAny  Transport = 8
)
```

<a name="URL"></a>

## type [URL](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/dburl.go#L59-L85>)

URL wraps the standard [net/url.URL](<https://pkg.go.dev/net/url/#URL>) type, adding OriginalScheme, Transport, Driver, Unaliased, and DSN strings.

```go
type URL struct {
    // URL is the base [net/url.URL].
    url.URL
    // OriginalScheme is the original parsed scheme (ie, "sq", "mysql+unix", "sap", etc).
    OriginalScheme string
    // Transport is the specified transport protocol (ie, "tcp", "udp",
    // "unix", ...), if provided.
    Transport string
    // Driver is the non-aliased SQL driver name that should be used in a call
    // to [sql.Open].
    Driver string
    // GoDriver is the Go SQL driver name to use when opening a connection to
    // the database. Used by Microsoft SQL Server's azuresql:// URLs, as the
    // wire-compatible alias style uses a different syntax style.
    GoDriver string
    // UnaliasedDriver is the unaliased driver name.
    UnaliasedDriver string
    // DSN is the built connection "data source name" that can be used in a
    // call to [sql.Open].
    DSN string
    // contains filtered or unexported fields
}
```

<a name="FromMap"></a>

### func [FromMap](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/dburl.go#L194>)

```go
func FromMap(components map[string]any) (*URL, error)
```

FromMap creates a [URL](<#URL>) using the mapped components.

Recognized components are:

```
protocol, proto, scheme
transport
username, user
password, pass
hostname, host
port
path, file, opaque
database, dbname, db
instance
parameters, params, options, opts, query, q
```

See [BuildURL](<#BuildURL>) for more information.

<a name="Parse"></a>

### func [Parse](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/dburl.go#L95>)

```go
func Parse(urlstr string) (*URL, error)
```

Parse parses a URL string, similar to the standard [net/url.Parse](<https://pkg.go.dev/net/url/#Parse>).

Handles parsing OriginalScheme, Transport, Driver, Unaliased, and DSN fields.

Note: if the URL has a Opaque component \(ie, URLs not specified as "scheme://" but "scheme:"\), and the database scheme does not support opaque components, Parse will attempt to re\-process the URL as "scheme://\<opaque\>".

<a name="URL.Normalize"></a>

### func \(\*URL\) [Normalize](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/dburl.go#L252>)

```go
func (u *URL) Normalize(sep, empty string, cut int) string
```

Normalize returns the driver, host, port, database, and user name of a URL, joined with sep, populating blank fields with empty.

<a name="URL.Short"></a>

### func \(\*URL\) [Short](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/dburl.go#L218>)

```go
func (u *URL) Short() string
```

Short provides a short description of the user, host, and database.

## Index

# passfile

```go
import "github.com/xo/dburl/passfile"
```

Package passfile provides a mechanism for reading database credentials from passfiles.

<details><summary>Example (Entries)</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
 "log"
 "os/user"

 "github.com/xo/dburl/passfile"
)

func main() {
 u, err := user.Current()
 if err != nil {
  log.Fatal(err)
 }
 // read ~/.usqlpass or $ENV{USQLPASS}
 entries, err := passfile.Entries(u.HomeDir, "usqlpass")
 if err != nil {
  log.Fatal(err)
 }
 for i, entry := range entries {
  log.Printf("%d: %v", i, entry)
 }
}
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Match)</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
 "log"
 "os/user"

 "github.com/xo/dburl"
 "github.com/xo/dburl/passfile"
)

func main() {
 v, err := user.Current()
 if err != nil {
  log.Fatal(err)
 }
 u, err := dburl.Parse("pg://")
 if err != nil {
  log.Fatal(err)
 }
 // read ~/.usqlpass or $ENV{USQLPASS}
 user, err := passfile.Match(u, v.HomeDir, "usqlpass")
 if err == nil {
  u.User = user
 }
 log.Println("url:", u.String())
}
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Expand"></a>

## func [Expand](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/passfile/passfile.go#L194>)

```go
func Expand(homeDir string, file string) string
```

Expand expands the beginning tilde \(\~\) in a file name to the provided home directory.

<a name="Match"></a>

## func [Match](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/passfile/passfile.go#L160>)

```go
func Match(u *dburl.URL, homeDir, name string) (*url.Userinfo, error)
```

Match returns a Userinfo from a passfile entry matching database URL read from the file in $HOME/.\<name\> or $ENV\{NAME\}.

Equivalent to MatchFile\(u, Path\(homeDir, name\), dburl.Protocols\(u.Driver\)...\).

<a name="MatchEntries"></a>

## func [MatchEntries](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/passfile/passfile.go#L112>)

```go
func MatchEntries(u *dburl.URL, entries []Entry, protocols ...string) (*url.Userinfo, error)
```

MatchEntries returns a Userinfo when the normalized v is found in entries.

<a name="MatchFile"></a>

## func [MatchFile](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/passfile/passfile.go#L141>)

```go
func MatchFile(u *dburl.URL, file string, protocols ...string) (*url.Userinfo, error)
```

MatchFile returns a Userinfo from a passfile entry matching database URL v read from the specified file.

<a name="MatchProtocols"></a>

## func [MatchProtocols](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/passfile/passfile.go#L169>)

```go
func MatchProtocols(u *dburl.URL, homeDir, name string, protocols ...string) (*url.Userinfo, error)
```

MatchProtocols returns a Userinfo from a passfile entry matching database URL read from the file in $HOME/.\<name\> or $ENV\{NAME\} using the specified protocols.

Equivalent to MatchFile\(u, Path\(homeDir, name\), protocols...\).

<a name="Open"></a>

## func [Open](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/passfile/passfile.go#L222>)

```go
func Open(urlstr, homeDir, name string) (*sql.DB, error)
```

Open opens a database connection for a URL, reading the named passfile in the home directory.

<a name="OpenURL"></a>

## func [OpenURL](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/passfile/passfile.go#L206>)

```go
func OpenURL(u *dburl.URL, homeDir, name string) (*sql.DB, error)
```

OpenURL opens a database connection for the provided URL, reading the named passfile in the home directory.

<a name="Path"></a>

## func [Path](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/passfile/passfile.go#L184>)

```go
func Path(homeDir, name string) string
```

Path returns the expanded path to the password file for name.

Uses $HOME/.\<name\>, overridden by environment variable $ENV\{NAME\} \(for example, \~/.usqlpass and $ENV\{USQLPASS\}\).

<a name="EmptyFieldError"></a>

## type [EmptyFieldError](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/passfile/passfile.go#L274-L277>)

EmptyFieldError is the empty field error.

```go
type EmptyFieldError struct {
    Line  int
    Field int
}
```

<a name="EmptyFieldError.Error"></a>

### func \(\*EmptyFieldError\) [Error](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/passfile/passfile.go#L280>)

```go
func (err *EmptyFieldError) Error() string
```

Error satisfies the error interface.

<a name="Entry"></a>

## type [Entry](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/passfile/passfile.go#L24-L26>)

Entry is a passfile entry.

Corresponds to a non\-empty line in a passfile.

```go
type Entry struct {
    Protocol, Host, Port, DBName, Username, Password string
}
```

<a name="Entries"></a>

### func [Entries](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/passfile/passfile.go#L176>)

```go
func Entries(homeDir, name string) ([]Entry, error)
```

Entries returns the entries for the specified passfile name.

Equivalent to ParseFile\(Path\(homeDir, name\)\).

<a name="NewEntry"></a>

### func [NewEntry](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/passfile/passfile.go#L29>)

```go
func NewEntry(v []string) Entry
```

NewEntry creates a new passfile entry.

<a name="Parse"></a>

### func [Parse](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/passfile/passfile.go#L43>)

```go
func Parse(r io.Reader) ([]Entry, error)
```

Parse parses passfile entries from the reader.

<a name="ParseFile"></a>

### func [ParseFile](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/passfile/passfile.go#L73>)

```go
func ParseFile(file string) ([]Entry, error)
```

ParseFile parses passfile entries contained in file.

<a name="Entry.Equals"></a>

### func \(Entry\) [Equals](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/passfile/passfile.go#L105>)

```go
func (entry Entry) Equals(v Entry, protocols ...string) bool
```

Equals returns true when v matches the entry.

<a name="Error"></a>

## type [Error](<https://github.com/xo/dburl/blob/master/passfile/passfile.go#L231>)

Error is a error.

```go
type Error string
```

<a name="ErrUnableToNormalizeURL"></a>

```go
const (
    // ErrUnableToNormalizeURL is the unable to normalize URL error.
    ErrUnableToNormalizeURL Error = "unable to normalize URL"
    // ErrMustNotBeDirectory is the must not be directory error.
    ErrMustNotBeDirectory Error = "must not be directory"
    // ErrHasGroupOrWorldAccess is the has group or world access error.
    ErrHasGroupOrWorldAccess Error = "has group or world access"
)
```


Error satisfies the error interface.
